message:
fix(fr-store): function_load enforces upstream charset on per-function names (br-frankenredis-r85v)

Self-review caught a seventh real defect: function_load applied
the [A-Za-z0-9_] charset gate to LIBRARY names but not the
per-function names extracted from register_function calls.
Upstream functions.c::functionLibCreateFunction (line 249)
applies functionsVerifyName to both, rejecting forged calls like
redis.register_function('bad-name', ...) with the same canonical
wording.

Add the per-function gate inside the parse loop so a malformed
register_function call aborts the entire load (matching upstream's
'commit nothing on any error' guarantee that ACLSetUser/ACL
SETUSER share — function_load is similarly all-or-nothing because
the library is only inserted into the store after the loop).

New test function_load_rejects_invalid_function_name_chars pins
the behaviour and confirms the library is not registered when
any function name fails the gate.

fr-store 296 passed; fr-command 656 passed; cargo clippy --tests
-D warnings clean; cargo fmt --check clean.

Slice of br-frankenredis-r85v.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
